scenarios_reviewed: Section C correction for bank fee remains straightforward but still depends on COA presence.
Internal transfer row suggests profile maintenance should happen earlier than review if accountant already confirmed the relationship in Coordinator.
Split Costco row highlights unresolved original-row superseded semantics.
No real Section A or Section B rows were available, so rule-error and AI-history-correction paths remain only partially exercised.
recommended_followups: Provide real profile/rules/observations/COA inputs and rerun to exercise Sections A and B.
Make transaction identifiers globally unique before any stateful coordinator or review workflow.
Document how intervention logging timestamps should differ from transaction dates.

interface_issues.description: Because the run populated only Section C mock rows, the review agent could not truly exercise rule-error or AI-high-confidence correction paths.
interface_issues.upstream_provided: Report draft with empty Section A/B and populated Section C only.
interface_issues.expected: A mixed report covering deterministic, AI, and accountant-confirmed sections.
interface_issues.impact: Review flow coverage is incomplete for the highest-value historical-correction paths.

spec_ambiguities.description: Coordinator can surface a profile change signal and effectively resolve a transfer, while Review Agent is described as advisory-only on profile changes; this division remains awkward.
spec_ambiguities.spec_text: Review Agent 不修改 profile.md ... Coordinator 会直接更新 profile 并触发 retrigger
spec_ambiguities.interpretation_a: Coordinator owns operational profile changes; Review only flags them retrospectively.
spec_ambiguities.interpretation_b: Both agents should follow the same profile-change policy for user consistency.
spec_ambiguities.recommendation: Unify or explicitly differentiate the profile-change contract between Coordinator and Review Agent.

spec_ambiguities.description: The spec still underdefines how split-transaction interventions should reference and preserve the original source transaction in intervention logs and rebuilt reports.
spec_ambiguities.spec_text: split / rebuild 相关流程依赖 Transaction Log 为 source of truth
spec_ambiguities.interpretation_a: Original row remains as superseded metadata.
spec_ambiguities.interpretation_b: Original row disappears from active reporting once children exist.
spec_ambiguities.recommendation: Define the canonical review/reporting visibility of superseded parent rows.

notes: This review step exercised workflow critique more than accounting correction because upstream automation never populated Sections A or B.
The report draft was still sufficient to surface workflow-level gaps around profile maintenance, split handling, and intervention logging.
